post.summary: CreateAccount mints a new account + initial key.
post.description:
Authorization: in `open` bootstrap mode, anonymous callers may
self-register. In `system_only` mode, only systems with `accounts.write`
may create accounts on a user's behalf. The proto allows BOTH; the
service handler rejects anonymous calls when bootstrap mode is
`system_only` with `REGISTRATION_DISABLED` (PERMISSION_DENIED).

patch.description:
Authorization: ACCOUNT-owns-resource (account self) OR System with
`accounts.admin`. Cross-account writes are rejected by the handler.

schema.description: Account is the publisher identity row. `id` is a UUIDv7.

get.summary:
StreamAccountArticles returns the per-account watermark feed in ASC
order. Each event corresponds to a publish, edit, current-version
redaction (which bumps `articles_live.updated_at`), or tombstone.
Drafts never appear. Ordering is `(event_at, article_id) ASC`.
get.description:
The stream **closes** on account deletion: the RPC surfaces
`ACCOUNT_DELETED` and republishers MUST remove all content from that
account on their target platform.

Authorization: System with `articles.stream`.

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description:
Opaque, base64-encoded JSON `{event_at, article_id}` cursor. Empty →
start from the beginning.

get.summary:
ListAccountArticles lists an account's articles in `created_at DESC`
order. Anonymous-readable.

post.summary: PublishArticle creates a new live article in one shot.
post.description:
Authorization: the Account whose id == request.account_id (so the
ACCOUNT_OWNS_RESOURCE class) OR a System with `articles.write`.

get.summary:
ListAccountDrafts lists an account's drafts, ordered by
`updated_at DESC`.
get.description:
Authorization: the Account whose id == request.account_id OR a
System with `drafts.read`.

post.summary: CreateDraft mints a new draft for the given account.
post.description:
Authorization: the Account whose id == request.account_id (so the
ACCOUNT_OWNS_RESOURCE class) OR a System with `drafts.write`.

get.summary:
ListAccountFollowers returns the followers of an account, ordered by
`created_at DESC`. `include_unfollowed=false` (default) hides
`unfollowed` rows. No filtering for deleted users — see `follows.md`.
get.description:
Authorization: the Account whose `id == request.account_id` OR a
System with `follows.read`.

post.summary: AddAccountKey registers an additional active key on an account.
post.description: Authorization: account self OR System with `accounts.admin`.

post.summary: RevokeAccountKey marks an existing key as revoked.
post.description:
Authorization: account self OR System with `accounts.admin`.
Lockout protection: the handler refuses to revoke the last active key
unless the caller is a System with `admin.*` scope (operator rescue).

get.summary:
GetAccount returns the full account row, including soft-deleted rows
(status=DELETED, 200 — never NOT_FOUND). Anonymous-readable per `auth.md`.

GetArticle returns the full article, including content nodes if live
and the current version is not redacted. Tombstoned articles return
200 with `state=ARTICLE_STATE_TOMBSTONE`. Anonymous-readable.

patch.summary:
EditArticle applies a field-masked edit, creating a new
article_versions row and bumping `articles_live.current_version`.
patch.description:
Authorization: the Account who owns the article OR System with
`articles.write`.

post.summary: TombstoneArticle is a one-way state transition (no untombstone).
post.description:
Authorization: the Account who owns the article OR System with
`articles.tombstone`.

patch.summary:
UpdateDraft applies a field-masked update to an existing draft. The
resulting record is re-validated strictly per `drafts.md`.
patch.description:
Authorization: the Account who owns the draft OR a System with
`drafts.write`.

get.summary:
GetDraft returns the full draft. Drafts are private working space; a
caller who does not own the draft (or a System with `drafts.read`)
receives `DRAFT_NOT_FOUND` rather than `PERMISSION_DENIED` — no
existence leak.

delete.summary:
DeleteDraft hard-deletes a draft (no tombstone — drafts were never
public).
delete.description:
Authorization: the Account who owns the draft OR a System with
`drafts.write`.

post.summary:
PublishDraft converts the draft to a live Article in a single
transaction, preserving the UUID.
post.description:
Authorization: the Account who owns the draft OR a System with
`articles.write` (this RPC produces an Article).

get.summary:
ListEvents — analytics-only. Filters AND-combined; cursor encodes
`(received_at, id)` for stable forward iteration; results in ASC order
so the ranker can drain incrementally.

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description: Opaque base64 cursor on `(received_at, id)` ASC.

post.summary:
RecordEvent appends a single event row. Server assigns `id` (UUIDv7) and
`received_at` (TSO); any client-supplied values for those fields are
silently ignored.
post.description:
Authorization: User whose `id == request.user_id` OR System with
`events.write`.

post.summary:
RecordEventBatch — all-or-none batch insert. Validation runs over the
whole batch first; on any failure the entire batch is rejected with the
first failing reason. On success the batch is inserted in a single tx.
Cap is `events.batch_max_items` (default 500).
post.description:
Authorization: same rule as `RecordEvent`, applied to **every** event in
the batch.

post.summary:
SendNotification — system-only writer. Authorization: System with
`notifications.send`.

SendNotificationBatch — system-only writer. Authorization: System with
`notifications.send`.
CreateUser mints a new user + initial key.
post.description:
Authorization: in `open` bootstrap mode, anonymous callers may
self-register. In `system_only` mode, only systems with `users.write`
may create users on a user's behalf. The proto allows BOTH; the
service handler rejects anonymous calls when bootstrap mode is
`system_only` with `REGISTRATION_DISABLED` (PERMISSION_DENIED).

get.summary:
GetUser returns the user row, including soft-deleted rows
(status=DELETED, 200 — never NOT_FOUND for self/system).
get.description:
Privacy: GetUser is NOT anonymous. Unauthorized callers receive
`USER_NOT_FOUND` (NOT_FOUND) — not PERMISSION_DENIED — so the API
does not leak user existence.

patch.description:
Authorization: USER_SELF OR System with `users.admin`. Cross-user
writes are rejected by the handler with `USER_NOT_FOUND`.

schema.description: User is the consumer identity row. `id` is a UUIDv7.

get.summary:
GetFollowFeed returns articles authored by accounts the user currently
follows, ordered by `articles.created_at DESC` with keyset pagination on
`(article.created_at, article.id)`. Articles from deleted accounts are
included; redacted current versions surface with `redacted=true` on the
summary. No "since I followed" cutoff — older articles surface too.
get.description:
Authorization: the User whose `id == request.user_id` OR a System with
`feeds.follow.read`.

get.summary:
GetRecommendationFeed returns the joined feed view for the user. The
join against `articles_live` automatically excludes tombstoned and
missing articles (see `feed-recommendation.md` for details).
get.description:
Authorization: the User whose `id == request.user_id` OR a System with
`feeds.recommendation.read`.

put.summary:
ReplaceRecommendationFeed atomically deletes the existing feed for the
user and inserts the supplied ordered list (position = index). System-only
by design — the ranker is the only writer.

get.summary:
ListUserFollows returns the follows owned by a user, ordered by
`created_at DESC`. `include_unfollowed=false` (default) hides
`unfollowed` rows.
get.description:
Authorization: the User whose `id == request.user_id` OR a System with
`follows.read`.

post.summary:
Follow creates or re-activates a `(user_id, account_id)` edge per
`follows.md` state machine. Idempotent in the active direction; a
previously `unfollowed` row resets `created_at = now`.
post.description:
Authorization: the User whose `id == request.user_id` OR a System with
`follows.write`. Cross-user attempts return `USER_NOT_FOUND`.

get.summary: GetFollow returns the edge regardless of status. Missing → `FOLLOW_NOT_FOUND`.
get.description:
Authorization: the User whose `id == request.user_id` OR a System with
`follows.read`.

delete.summary:
Unfollow flips an `active` edge to `unfollowed`. No-op success on an
already-unfollowed row; `FOLLOW_NOT_FOUND` if no edge has ever
existed.
delete.description:
Authorization: the User whose `id == request.user_id` OR a System with
`follows.write`.

post.summary: AddUserKey registers an additional active key on a user.
RevokeUserKey marks an existing key as revoked.
post.description:
Authorization: USER_SELF OR System with `users.admin`.
Lockout protection: the handler refuses to revoke the last active key
unless the caller is a System with `admin.*` scope (operator rescue).

draft.description:
Full Draft view, including content nodes.

`id` is a UUIDv7 minted on `CreateDraft`; the same UUID becomes
`articles.id` on `PublishDraft`.

articleIds.description:
Order is the feed order; position = index. Soft references — the ranker
may push article ids ahead of article ingestion.

headlinesV1Follow.description: Follow is one `(user, account)` edge per `docs/design/follows.md`.

v1Account.description: Account is the publisher identity row. `id` is a UUIDv7.

v1AccountStreamItem.description:
AccountStreamItem carries an `ArticleSummary` per `account-stream.md`. The
summary's `state` discriminator already distinguishes LIVE from TOMBSTONE,
so republishers consume one shape and dispatch on `state` (mirror to target
platform on LIVE; remove on TOMBSTONE). Body content is fetched separately
via `GetArticle` to honor the list-vs-view rule.

v1Article.description:
Full Article view, including content nodes when live.

`id` is a UUIDv7 used directly as the public URL identifier (no slug).

v1ArticleEdit.description:
Edit envelope used by EditArticle. Fields not selected by the caller's
FieldMask are ignored; see `docs/design/articles.md`.

content.description: Empty when the current version's content has been redacted.

redacted.description: True when the current version's content has been redacted.

v1ArticleSummary.description:
Compact view used by list/feed endpoints. Identical structure to `Article`
minus the content nodes; clients fetch `GetArticle` for the full body.

v1Draft.description:
Full Draft view, including content nodes.

`id` is a UUIDv7 minted on `CreateDraft`; the same UUID becomes
`articles.id` on `PublishDraft`.

v1DraftSummary.description:
Compact view used by list endpoints. No content nodes; clients fetch
`GetDraft` for the full body.

v1DwellProperties.description: DWELL — time spent reading. Caller may emit multiple per session.

v1Event.description:
Event — a single recorded activity row. `properties` oneof selector must
match `type`; the service rejects mismatches with `EVENT_TYPE_MISMATCH`.

v1EventType.description:
EventType — the v1 vocabulary for user-activity events.

`data-model.md` stores the discriminator as `text` so the wire enum and the
DB column move in lockstep; the SCREAMING_SNAKE name (e.g. `IMPRESSION`)
without the `EVENT_TYPE_` prefix is used as the DB string.

v1FeedItem.description:
FeedItem carries an `ArticleSummary` per `feed-recommendation.md` so list
callers don't pay for content nodes; clients call `GetArticle` for the body.

v1FollowFeedItem.description:
FollowFeedItem carries an `ArticleSummary` per `feed-follow.md`. Distinct
from `FeedItem` (recommendation) — there is no `position` field; ordering is
data-driven by `article.created_at DESC` and is not server-assigned.

v1ImpressionProperties.description: IMPRESSION — article shown in a feed.

v1LikeProperties.description: LIKE — user liked an article. No payload.

items.description: No content nodes; clients call GetArticle for the full body.

v1Node.description:
Node is a recursive structured payload mirroring Telegraph's Node tree.
Either a text leaf or an element with attributes and children.

v1OpenProperties.description: OPEN — user opened the article detail view.

v1PublicKey.description:
PublicKey carries an algorithm name plus the base64-encoded raw key bytes.
The exact format of `public_key` is defined by `algo` (e.g. for `ed25519`,
the bytes are the 32-byte raw public key, base64-encoded → 44 chars).

recorded.description: Same order as the request, populated with id + received_at.

v1UnlikeProperties.description: UNLIKE — user reverted a like. No payload.

v1User.description: User is the consumer identity row. `id` is a UUIDv7.
